name: Colorado (United States)

colors.symbolism: the reddish-brown soil of Colorado (the state's name is Spanish for 'red-coloured')
Colorado's abundant sunshine and gold mining heritage

description: Three equal horizontal bands — blue, white, blue. Across the centre, slightly toward the hoist, a large red letter 'C' partially encloses a golden-yellow disc.
history: Designed by Andrew Carlisle Johnson of the Daughters of the American Revolution and adopted by the Colorado General Assembly on 5 June 1911. Specifications for the proportions and exact shades of red and blue (matching the U.S. flag) were codified by acts of 1929 and 1964.

features.symbolism: blue stripes for the sky and white for the snow-capped mountains
the gold of Colorado's mining history and the state's abundant sunshine
